name: Assistant links a quote to an existing customer by name
description: User says 'another quote for Mrs. Park'. Assistant should resolve to the existing client record (not create a new one) and pre-fill name/email/phone on the draft.

persona.name: Lena Park
persona.context: Has worked with Mrs. Park (a different person — same last name) before. Three Park-family clients exist.

preconditions: Janet is the most recent contact

steps.action: Types: 'New quote for Park — touch up trim, $220.'
steps.expect: Assistant detects the ambiguity and asks 'Janet, Sue, or Mike Park?' with three quick-pick chips
Assistant proceeds with a create_quote tied to Janet's customerId — not a new customer record
Janet's card shows the new quote in her timeline; client count is unchanged

evaluationCriteria.check: Quick-pick chips are visually distinguishable from regular text and tappable on mobile
Disambiguation feels like one extra tap, not a long form
If only one match exists, the assistant skips the disambiguation and proceeds directly
Chips are real buttons with role=button and Enter/Space activates them
